steps.title: Spot the parenthetical interruption
steps.content: The relative clause starting with 'that' contains a parenthetical interruption: the phrase 'through community involvement and scientific ______' breaks off before the clause continues with 'aim to bring'.

steps.title: Apply the pairing rule
steps.content: Rule: paired em-dashes must open and close a parenthetical interruption. The sentence already has an opening em-dash after "that—", so the blank must close it with another em-dash. Reconstructed sentence with each choice: A) "scientific study," — a comma after the parenthetical does not match the opening em-dash; the pair is broken; eliminated. B) "scientific study—" — closes the em-dash pair opened after "that"; the sentence reads "efforts that—through community involvement and scientific study—aim to bring adjutants back"; correct. C) "scientific study:" — a colon cannot close an em-dash parenthetical; eliminated. D) "scientific study" — no closing punctuation leaves the opening em-dash unmatched; eliminated. Verdict: B is correct.
